**User:** nick.wood@ca.belfor.com | **Time:** 1m 20s
**Rooms:** 6 total (6 affected, 0 unaffected) | **Photos:** 0 | **Notes:** 1 | **Floor Plans:** 1
**Input Quality:** 2/5 Minimal | **Pipeline Health:** 4/5 Minor | **Issue Score:** 3/5 Moderate | **Overall:** 3.0/5

### What Was Provided

| Category | Status | Details |
|----------|--------|---------|
| Room Setup | Poor | 6 rooms; 0 in app, 1 from notes |
| Field Photos | None | 0 photos; 6 rooms without photos |
| Technician Notes | Minimal | 1 notes; 5 rooms without notes |
| Floor Plans | Partial | 1 plans; 0 rooms with measurements |
| Room Name Matching | Good | 0 unmatched floor plan rooms |
| Moisture Data | None | -- |
| Guidelines | None | -- |

### Input Assessment

No field photos were submitted for this scope. Notes were provided for some rooms (1 notes across 6 affected rooms), but not all rooms had written descriptions of the damage. 1 floor plan(s) were uploaded but room labels could not be matched, so measurements could not be assigned to specific rooms. Rooms were not set up in the app before running the scope, which means they will not sync back to the field application. No moisture monitoring data was submitted.

### Pipeline Assessment

The scope completed successfully in 1m 20s. Data quality flags: 6 affected room(s) had no photos; 5 affected room(s) had no technician notes.

### Issue Assessment

The system detected 3 data quality issue(s): 1 equipment sizing conflict(s); 2 affected room(s) without photos.

### Recommendations

1. Take photos of each affected room before running the scope. Photos help verify damage type and extent in each room.
2. Add notes for the 5 room(s) missing them. Even brief notes about damage type and extent help.
3. Set up rooms in the app before running the scope. Rooms created in the app sync back to your field application; rooms inferred from notes do not.

No data quality issues were detected in this scope.

1. Take photos of each affected room before running the scope. Photos help verify damage type and extent in each room.
2. Add technician notes describing the damage in each room. Notes provide details that photos alone cannot capture, like the source of water or hidden damage.
3. Upload a floor plan with room measurements. Without measurements, task quantities cannot be calculated and must be filled in manually.

---

## 93ce5bf5797963443cfcddac647db443 -- 2026-03-13 02:31 UTC -- v2026.09.3

**User:** scott.shizuru@us.belfor.com | **Time:** 6m 28s
**Rooms:** 38 total (38 affected, 0 unaffected) | **Photos:** 668 | **Notes:** 18 | **Floor Plans:** 9 | **Videos:** 11
**Input Quality:** 4/5 Good | **Pipeline Health:** 3/5 Moderate | **Issue Score:** 3/5 Moderate | **Overall:** 3.3/5

### What Was Provided

| Category | Status | Details |
|----------|--------|---------|
| Structures | Clean | 1 structural (Main Building) |
| Rooms | Good | 38 total; 38 affected, 0 unaffected; 0 organizational |
| Room Setup | Partial | 38 rooms; 26 from description, 12 from measurement images only |
| Field Photos | Partial | 668 photos; 478 high relevance, 145 medium, 45 low; uneven distribution (241 on 2nd Floor alone) |
| Thermal Images | 27 found | 27 thermal/FLIR images detected in photo set |
| 360 Photos | None | 0 panoramic/360 images detected |
| Video Transcripts | 11 found | 11 room video transcripts (Exterior: 3, 1st Floor: 8) |
| General Notes | None | No general notes section |
| Technician Notes | Limited | 1 of 17 rooms with substantive content; 16 empty placeholders |
| Floor Plans | Good | 6 floor plans (complete) + 9 measurement images |
| Room Name Matching | Issues | 24 matched, 14 unmatched (Basement Hall, Gym, Bedrooms 2-4, Bathrooms 2-5, etc.) |
| Moisture Data | None | No moisture readings despite Cat 3 / Class 3 |
| Guidelines | None | -- |

### Input Assessment
- Large-scale fire/water/mold loss on a 4,001 SF multi-level residential property with 1 structure (Main Building), spanning basement through 3rd floor
- 668 field photos provided, heavily concentrated: 241 tagged to "2nd Floor", 86 to "Floor 3", 72 to "Exterior"; several main-floor rooms received zero room-tagged photos
- 27 thermal images detected across the photo set, indicating FLIR documentation was performed
- 11 video transcripts provided across 2 room groupings (Exterior: 3, 1st Floor: 8); videos served as primary damage narrative
- Only 1 room (2nd Floor) had substantive technician note content; 16 other room note sections were empty placeholders
- 6 floor plans provided (all complete) plus 9 measurement images; however 14 of 38 rooms had no floor plan match
- No general notes, no guidelines, and no moisture reading data were provided
- 9 rooms missing measurements: Basement Hall, Gym, Bedroom 4, Bedroom 3, Bedroom 2, Bathroom 3, Bathroom 5, Floor 3, and Laundry Room

### Issue Assessment
- 9 rooms missing measurements -- task quantities show "TBD" for these rooms
- Significant photo-to-room mismatch: photos tagged to 17 app room names but pipeline identified 38 scope rooms; 21+ rooms show zero room-tagged photos
- Sparse technician notes: only 1 of 17 input rooms had written content; system relied heavily on video transcripts and photo analysis
- Floor plan room names partially mismatched: 14 rooms (Basement Hall, Hallways, Washroom, Gym, Carport, Bedrooms 2-4, Bathrooms 2-5, Stairwell 1, Floor 3) had no floor plan match
- 12 rooms sourced from measurement images have measurements but zero notes, zero photos, and no damage descriptions
- Duplicate room names: "Carport", "Hall", "Bathroom", and "Bedroom" each appear on multiple floors
- No moisture data provided despite Category 3 / Class 3 classification

### Recommendations
1. **Verify floor plan room names match claim room names.** 14 of 38 rooms couldn't be matched to floor plan data. Editing the floor plan room labels to match (e.g., "BR1" to "Bedroom 1") and re-running the scope would assign measurements to all rooms automatically.
2. **Provide measurements for the 9 TBD rooms.** Basement Hall, Gym, Bedrooms 2-4, Bathrooms 3 and 5, Floor 3 need dimensions for accurate task quantities.
3. **Reconcile photo room tagging with scope rooms.** 21+ scope rooms show zero tagged photos; re-tagging photos to match actual room names would improve coverage.
4. **Add technician notes for key affected rooms.** The primary fire room (Bedroom 4) had minimal note content; rooms with direct fire or structural damage warrant detailed written observations.
5. **Supply moisture readings.** Category 3 / Class 3 loss requires documented moisture data to validate drying chamber design and equipment quantities.

### Pipeline Assessment
- Completed in approximately 6.5 minutes for 38 rooms, 668 photos, and 11 video transcripts
- Photo analysis classified 478 images as high relevance, 145 as medium, 45 as low; 27 thermal images identified
- Mold safety alert correctly triggered with scope items added (antimicrobial, containment, HEPA, air scrubbing)
- Asbestos advisory correctly flagged as testing-only with no premature scope items
- Equipment calculations: 8 dehumidifiers (913 PPD), 13 AFDs, 150-192 air movers; fire-specific: 4 ozone generators, 7 thermal foggers
- All 38 rooms classified as affected with 4,001 SF total -- consistent with full-building fire suppression water scenario
- No IICRC conflicts, material conflicts, or scope conflicts detected
- 12 rooms added from measurement images had no corresponding description or photo context

### Bug Assessment

| EA # | Rule | Priority | Result | Node | Details |
|------|------|----------|--------|------|---------|
| -- | water_category | -- | PASS | -- | -- |
| -- | water_class | -- | PASS | -- | -- |
| EA-1 | room_integrity | HIGH | **FAIL** | Merge | 5 orphaned room names in materials not in rooms array |
| EA-2 | hallucination | HIGH | **FAIL** | Merge | Fabricated material-room associations for non-existent rooms |
| EA-3 | task_sequence | LOW | **FAIL** | Tasks | Parenthetical annotations in affected_materials |
| -- | equipment | -- | PASS | -- | -- |
| -- | task_tense | -- | PASS | -- | -- |
| EA-4 | material_room_integrity | HIGH | **FAIL** | Merge | 5 room names in materials don't exist in rooms array |

**Rules Checked:** 8/8 | **Bugs Found:** 4

**Full evaluation (19 rules):** 11/19 — see `joe/evals/traces/trace_93ce5bf5.md`

**Rooms:** 3 total (2 affected, 1 unaffected) | **Photos:** 55 | **Notes:** 4 | **Floor Plans:** 1
**Input Quality:** 4/5 Good | **Pipeline Health:** 4/5 Good | **Issue Score:** 3/5 Moderate | **Overall:** 3.7/5

### What Was Provided

| Category | Status | Details |
|----------|--------|---------|
| Structures | Mixed | 5 total; 1 structural (Initial Inspection), 4 organizational (Content, Repair, Emergency, Sketch & Scope) |
| Rooms | Good | 3 total; 2 affected (Storage, Mens Bathroom), 1 unaffected (Exterior); 0 organizational |
| Room Setup | Good | 3 rooms; all in app |
| Field Photos | Good | 55 photos; Mens Bathroom: 21, Storage: 20, Exterior: 14 |
| Thermal Images | None | 0 thermal/FLIR images detected |
| 360 Photos | None | 0 panoramic/360 images detected |

| Room Name Matching | Issues | Floor plan lists "Storage Room" but system uses "Storage"; "Mens Bathroom" absent from floor plan |
| Moisture Data | Present | 2 drying chambers configured with dehu/air mover calculations |
| Guidelines | None | -- |

### Input Assessment
- Commercial basement property with ground water intrusion through ICF foundation; 83-hour gap between intrusion and scope
- 2 affected rooms (Storage 46 SF, Mens Bathroom 192 SF) plus 1 unaffected Exterior room; total affected area 238 SF
- Water Category 3 (ground water), Class 2; visible mold growth and biological contamination in both affected rooms
- 55 field photos with good per-room coverage (20+ photos each for affected rooms)
- 4 technician notes with detailed scope instructions including drywall heights, material callouts, and containment requirements
- 1 complete floor plan provided but room name matching is partial -- "Storage Room" on floor plan vs "Storage" in system; "Mens Bathroom" absent from floor plan entirely
- Moisture monitoring data present with drying chamber calculations for both rooms
- No thermal images, video transcripts, or external guidelines provided

### Issue Assessment
- Floor plan room name mismatch: "Storage Room" normalized to "Storage", "Mens Bathroom" not listed on floor plan -- measurements fell back to moisture monitoring dimensions
- Technician COL note lists category as "2/3" while system resolved to Category 3; conservative resolution is correct for ground water per IICRC S500
- Both rooms show "No matching validated floor plan measurements" -- floor plan names did not align with claim room names for measurement validation

### Recommendations
1. **Verify floor plan room names match claim room names.** The floor plan lists "Storage Room" but the system expects "Storage"; "Mens Bathroom" is absent entirely. Editing the floor plan labels to match would enable validated measurements instead of fallback dimensions.
2. **Add thermal imaging.** No thermal images for either affected room. Given Cat 3 ground water with 83 hours of exposure and visible mold, thermal scans would confirm moisture extent behind walls.
3. **Verify room dimensions.** Both rooms lack explicit L x W dimensions in claim data. Floor plan measurement validation would strengthen the scope.
4. **Consider video documentation.** No room video walkthroughs were provided. Video would help confirm extent of mold growth and material conditions.

### Pipeline Assessment
- Completed in 1 minute 54 seconds; 15 LLM generation calls across the pipeline
- System correctly applied IICRC S500 standards with material restorability driving removal vs clean-and-disinfect decisions
- Equipment: 2 dehumidifiers (120 PPD each), 2 AFDs (500 CFM HEPA with carbon), 7-9 air movers across 2 drying chambers
- Bacteria safety alert generated with appropriate PPE requirements (P100 respirator, double nitrile gloves, coveralls)
- No scope conflicts or material mismatches detected

**Rooms:** 18 total (7 affected, 11 unaffected) | **Photos:** 316 | **Notes:** 23 | **Floor Plans:** 3
**Input Quality:** 4/5 Good | **Pipeline Health:** 4/5 Good | **Issue Score:** 3/5 Moderate | **Overall:** 3.7/5

### What Was Provided

| Category | Status | Details |
|----------|--------|---------|
| Structures | Mixed | 6 total; 1 structural (Main Building), 5 organizational |
| Rooms | Good | 18 total; 7 affected, 11 unaffected; 0 organizational |

| 360 Photos | None | 0 panoramic/360 images detected |
| Video Transcripts | None | 0 room video transcripts |
| General Notes | None | No general notes section |

| Room Name Matching | Good | Floor plan rooms matched to scope rooms; multi-unit naming handled correctly |
| Moisture Data | None | Moisture Readings room exists but no data provided |
| Guidelines | None | -- |

### Input Assessment
- Multi-family residential property (7-unit apartment building); ice damming suspected as cause of loss
- 316 photos provided extensive visual evidence across Units 3, 4, and common areas; zero thermal images limits hidden moisture confirmation
- 3 complete floor plans (Unit 3, Unit 4, Hallway Common) provide full dimensional coverage for affected units
- 23 technician notes delivered detailed room-by-room scope instructions including drywall dimensions, fixture lists, and affected/unaffected status
- No moisture readings submitted despite a dedicated "Moisture Readings" room existing in the app
- No video transcripts, general notes, or external guidelines provided
- Water Category 2, Class 2; ~34 hours from loss to scope

### Issue Assessment
- Smoke reference in drying/equipment notes references "smoke-affected rooms" in a water-only loss -- appears to be a template artifact or hallucination in the equipment generation step
- Unit 3 Entry marked unaffected but has damage summary describing ceiling drywall warping and water staining -- technician note says "Entry not affected / Floor protection only", so classification is correct per technician intent but language is misleading
- Missing baseboard removal tasks in several rooms where wall drywall removal is scoped
- Photo aggregation gap: only 14 of 316 images produced room-level analyses; temporal classification returned "unknown" for all 14

### Recommendations
1. **Add thermal imaging.** Zero thermal images for a multi-unit ice damming loss with ceiling and wall cavity involvement -- thermal scans would confirm hidden moisture behind intact finishes.
2. **Submit moisture readings.** The empty Moisture Readings room should be populated to substantiate the Cat 2 / Class 2 assignment and document baseline vs dry-standard values.
3. **Clarify Unit 3 Entry scope language.** The damage summary reads as if active damage is present while technician intent was protection-only -- this could cause field confusion.
4. **Review baseboard task consistency.** Where wall drywall is being removed to floor line, ensure baseboard removal is explicitly scoped.
5. **Verify equipment counts.** 36-44 air movers across 853 affected SF yields ~1 mover per 19-24 SF, within IICRC Class 2 guidance.

### Pipeline Assessment
- Completed in 3 minutes 24 seconds for 316 photos, 23 notes, 18 rooms
- IICRC S500:2021 correctly identified as primary standard; S520 and S700 correctly excluded
- Category 2 assignment well-reasoned citing exterior water migration; Class 2 justified by moderate evaporation load
- Equipment: 2 dehumidifiers (120 PPD), 36-44 air movers, 1 drying chamber
- Floor plan room matching handled multi-unit naming (Unit 3, Unit 4, Common Area) correctly
- One drying chamber consolidates all 7 affected rooms across two units and common area

## 08bba5a08a10c2967cad19fcb6b04a80 -- 2026-03-13 00:26 UTC -- v2026.09.3

**User:** tammy.gemmill@ca.belfor.com | **Time:** 2m 47s
**Rooms:** 18 total (6 affected, 12 unaffected) | **Photos:** 316 | **Notes:** 20 | **Floor Plans:** 3 | **Videos:** 0
**Input Quality:** 4/5 Good | **Pipeline Health:** 4/5 Minor | **Issue Score:** 4/5 Minor | **Overall:** 4.0/5
**Water Category:** 1 | **Water Class:** 2 | **Source of Loss:** Suspected ice damming with water intrusion in Unit 4 ceiling and walls
**Total SF:** 1,771 | **Affected SF:** 650 | **Unaffected SF:** 1,120

### What Was Provided

| Category | Status | Details |
|----------|--------|---------|
| Structures | Clean | 6 structures; 1 structural (Main Building), 5 organizational (Repair, Content, Emergency, Sketch & Scope, Initial Inspection) |
| Rooms | Good | 18 total; 6 affected, 12 unaffected; 0 organizational |
| Room Setup | Good | 18 rooms; 10 in app, 10 from notes; rooms span Unit 4, Unit 3, Common Areas, 1st Floor |
| Field Photos | Good | 316 photos across 14 rooms; Unit 3 parent room 85, Unit 4 parent room 73, Unit 3 Living Room 31, Unit 4 Living Room 26, Exterior 24, Unit 3 Kitchen 16, Unit 4 Bedroom 15, Common Hallway 12, Unit 4 Bathroom 12, Unit 4 Kitchen 9, Unit 3 Entry 5, Unit 3 Bedroom 5, Unit 3 Bathroom 2, Unit 4 Entry 1 |
| Thermal Images | None | 0 thermal/FLIR images detected in photo set |
| 360 Photos | None | 0 panoramic/360 images detected |
| Video Transcripts | None | 0 videos |
| General Notes | Present | Initial job details note with contact info and ice damming description |
| Technician Notes | Detailed | 20 notes across rooms; detailed scope instructions and measurements for Unit 4 and Unit 3 rooms; 2 affected rooms without notes |
| Floor Plans | Good | 3 floor plans (Unit 4, Unit 3, Hallway Common) all complete status; measurements provided via notes |
| Room Name Matching | Good | 7 rooms matched by ID (Entry, Kitchen, Living Room, Bedroom, Bathroom, Living Room And Kitchen, Common Hallway); 0 unmatched |
| Moisture Data | None | No moisture readings provided despite active water intrusion |
| Guidelines | S500 | IICRC S500:2021 active; S520/S700 not applicable |

### Input Assessment
- Multi-unit residential ice damming loss at 27 Cockburn St, Perth -- 7-unit apartment building with water intrusion through roofline into Unit 4 and Unit 3 ceilings and walls
- 316 photos heavily concentrated on parent rooms: Unit 3 (85 photos) and Unit 4 (73 photos) dominate the set; room-specific photos are well-distributed for affected areas
- No thermal images were captured despite active water intrusion requiring moisture mapping; this limits verification of water migration extent behind walls
- Technician notes are thorough with explicit scope instructions ("remove and replace drywall", "cover up cabinets 8' LF + 6' LF") and room-by-room measurements
- Technician explicitly instructs "do this estimate as remove and replace, due to tenant and contents will have to be done all at once" across Unit 4 rooms
- Unit 3 notes specify separate emergency and repair estimates; bathroom and bedroom marked unaffected
- 3 floor plans all complete; measurements provided through notes rather than floor plan data, which the pipeline was able to consume
- No moisture readings provided despite 32-hour active water intrusion -- a gap for verifying drying progress
- Date of Loss: March 10, 2026; work started March 11, 2026 at 8:15 AM; inspection at 8:26 PM on March 12, 2026 (after hours)

### Issue Assessment
- Pipeline assigned Category 1 (clean water from ice/snow melt) and Class 2 (significant but not extreme absorption); reasonable given the ice damming source and ceiling-focused damage
- Unit 3 Kitchen and Living Room merged into a single "Living Room And Kitchen" room -- this follows the note structure but collapses two distinct floor plan rooms into one
- Flooring material mismatch detected: Unit 4 Bedroom listed as Laminate in materials array but Vinyl in room elements; resolved in favor of Vinyl per photo confirmation
- Entry room in Unit 4 marked affected with protection-only scope but no visible water damage in photos -- pipeline correctly respected the user instruction
- Unit 4 Bathroom marked unaffected per technician note ("un affected") but still included in the affected room list in some pipeline outputs
- Common Hallway, Unit 3 Entry, and Unit 3 Bedroom flagged as having few photos (2-5 each)
- Emergency inspection service correctly flagged as after-hours (Yes)
- PPE set to standard (nitrile gloves, standard work attire) appropriate for Category 1

### Recommendations
1. Capture thermal/FLIR images of ceilings and upper walls in all affected rooms. Thermal imaging is critical for verifying water migration paths behind intact drywall, especially with ice damming where water can travel laterally before showing surface damage.
2. Add moisture readings for all affected rooms. With a 32-hour intrusion timeline and active drying, moisture data is essential for documenting initial conditions and tracking drying progress per IICRC S500 requirements.
3. Add technician notes for the 2 affected rooms currently missing them. Even brief damage descriptions help the pipeline produce more accurate scopes.
4. Consider adding photos to low-coverage rooms (Common Hallway 12, Unit 4 Entry 1, Unit 3 Bathroom 2). The Entry room has only 1 photo despite being in scope for protection.
5. Review the merged Living Room And Kitchen for Unit 3 -- if these are distinct rooms in the floor plan, providing separate notes per room may produce more accurate per-room task quantities.

### Pipeline Assessment
- Scope completed successfully in 2m 47s with 14 processing steps
- Single drying chamber (DC1) covering all 6 affected rooms at 650 SF: 2 dehumidifiers (120 PPD each, 123 PPD required), 21-25 air movers
- Equipment sizing appears adequate for the affected area; dehumidifier capacity closely matches the calculated requirement
- Wall damage percentages assigned: Living Room 35%, Bedroom 30%, Living Room And Kitchen 30%, Kitchen 10%, Common Hallway 10%, Entry 0%
- Specialty drying recommended for hardwood flooring in 6 rooms -- appropriate given low-evaporation materials present
- No air filtration needed (consistent with Category 1 clean water)
- No scope conflicts or IICRC standard conflicts detected
- Measurement validation passed with 245.7 SF total area discrepancy (acceptable for note-sourced measurements)
- All affected rooms have measurements; no rooms missing dimensional data

**Input Quality:** 4/5 Good | **Pipeline Health:** 4/5 Minor | **Issue Score:** 3/5 Moderate | **Overall:** 3.7/5
**Water Category:** 2 | **Water Class:** 3 | **Source of Loss:** Suspected ice damming affecting ceilings and exterior walls in Unit 4 and common hallway areas
**Total SF:** 1,817 | **Affected SF:** 709 | **Unaffected SF:** 1,108

### What Was Provided

| Category | Status | Details |
|----------|--------|---------|
| Structures | Clean | 6 structures; 1 structural (Main Building), 5 organizational (Repair, Content, Emergency, Sketch & Scope, Initial Inspection) |
| Rooms | Good | 20 total; 8 affected, 12 unaffected; 0 organizational |
| Room Setup | Good | 20 rooms; 12 in app, 11 from notes; rooms span Unit 4, Unit 3, Common Area, Exterior, Main Floor, 1st Floor |
| Field Photos | Good | 316 photos across 14 rooms; Unit 3 parent room 85, Unit 4 parent room 73, Unit 3 Living Room 31, Unit 4 Living Room 26, Exterior 24, Unit 3 Kitchen 16, Unit 4 Bedroom 15, Common Hallway 12, Unit 4 Bathroom 12, Unit 4 Kitchen 9, Unit 3 Entry 5, Unit 3 Bedroom 5, Unit 3 Bathroom 2, Unit 4 Entry 1 |
| Thermal Images | None | 0 thermal/FLIR images detected in photo set |
| 360 Photos | None | 0 panoramic/360 images detected |
| Video Transcripts | None | 0 videos |
| General Notes | Present | Initial job details note with contact info and ice damming description |
| Technician Notes | Detailed | 20 notes across rooms; detailed scope instructions and measurements; all rooms have notes |
| Floor Plans | Good | 3 floor plans (Unit 4, Unit 3, Hallway Common) all complete status; measurements provided via notes |
| Room Name Matching | Partial | 5 rooms matched by ID (Entry, Kitchen, Living Room, Bedroom, Bathroom); 2 matched by name (Hallway, Exterior); Hallway and Exterior missing measurements |
| Moisture Data | None | No moisture readings provided despite active water intrusion |
| Guidelines | S500 | IICRC S500:2021 active; S520/S700 not applicable; mold Condition 1 flagged (testing recommended) |

### Input Assessment
- Same job as Trace 4 (08bba5a0...) -- identical input data (316 photos, 3 floor plans, 20 notes, same user, same property at 27 Cockburn St, Perth)
- This trace ran 1 minute 14 seconds before Trace 4, both submitted within the same session window (00:25 vs 00:26 UTC)
- Multi-unit residential ice damming loss -- 7-unit apartment building with water intrusion through roofline into Unit 4, Unit 3, and common areas
- 316 photos with identical distribution as Trace 4; no thermal images despite active water intrusion
- Technician notes are thorough with explicit scope instructions and room-by-room measurements
- 3 floor plans all complete; measurements sourced primarily from notes
- No moisture readings provided despite 32-hour active water intrusion

### Issue Assessment
- **Category/Class divergence from Trace 4:** This run assigned Category 2 / Class 3 vs Trace 4's Category 1 / Class 2 -- a significant difference from identical input data
  - Category 2 justification cited "non-potable sources with potential exposure to exterior contaminants (roof materials, wall cavities, and run-off)" -- a more conservative interpretation of ice dam water
  - Class 3 justified by "high evaporation load" across multiple ceiling areas -- a reasonable but more aggressive classification than Trace 4's Class 2
  - This inconsistency is a notable pipeline stability concern: same input producing different category/class assignments
- **Mold flagged in this run but not Trace 4:** Condition 1 mold assessment with testing recommended, despite no visible mold growth reported; Trace 4 had no mold details at all
- **Enhanced PPE:** N95 respirator, safety glasses, coveralls, rubber boots, boot covers -- appropriate for Category 2 but different from Trace 4's standard PPE
- **Room count difference:** 20 rooms vs Trace 4's 18 -- this run separated Unit 3 Living Room and Kitchen into distinct rooms (IDs 8 and 9) and added Exterior (ID 12), while Trace 4 merged them into "Living Room And Kitchen" (ID 8)
- **Affected room count:** 8 affected here vs 6 in Trace 4 -- the Exterior room and separate Unit 3 Kitchen are additionally marked affected
- **Missing measurements:** Hallway and Exterior rooms lack dimensions, producing TBD quantities in the final scope; Trace 4 had all measurements resolved
- **Measurement validation failed** due to missing Hallway and Exterior dimensions; total area discrepancy of 229.2 SF
- **Air filtration needed:** This run flagged air filtration for the drying chamber (consistent with Category 2); Trace 4 did not
- **Emergency inspection not flagged after-hours:** Listed as "No" despite the inspection occurring at 8:25 PM; Trace 4 correctly flagged it as "Yes"
- **Antimicrobial treatment included:** Per-room tasks include "clean and decontaminate" and "apply antimicrobial" -- appropriate for Category 2 but absent from Trace 4
- **Dehumidifier undersizing:** 2 units at 120 PPD each (240 PPD total) but 178 PPD required; closer match than Trace 4's 123 PPD requirement

### Recommendations
1. **Investigate Category/Class instability:** Two runs on identical data produced Cat 1/Class 2 vs Cat 2/Class 3 -- this is the most significant finding. The pipeline's LLM-based classification is non-deterministic on borderline cases like ice damming. Consider adding a deterministic override or confidence threshold for water source classification.
2. Capture thermal/FLIR images of ceilings and upper walls in all affected rooms. Thermal imaging would help resolve the Category ambiguity by showing whether water contacted exterior contaminants.
3. Add moisture readings for all affected rooms. With a 32-hour intrusion timeline, moisture data is essential for documenting initial conditions and would help justify category/class assignments.
4. Provide measurements for the Common Hallway room. This run lost hallway dimensions that Trace 4 successfully captured, resulting in TBD quantities in the final scope output.
5. Review whether Exterior should be scoped as an affected room. The exterior photos show building envelope damage (missing shingles, stained siding) but exterior repairs are typically handled separately from interior mitigation scope.
6. Confirm after-hours status: the 8:25 PM inspection should flag as after-hours work.

### Pipeline Assessment
- Scope completed successfully in 3m 18s with 14 processing steps (31 seconds slower than Trace 4 on identical input)
- Single drying chamber (DC1) covering 8 affected rooms at 709 SF: 2 dehumidifiers (120 PPD each, 178 PPD required), 23-27 air movers
- Air filtration flagged as needed (Category 2 contamination risk) -- a difference from Trace 4's no-filtration result
- Wall damage percentages: Living Room 50%, Bedroom 30%, Hallway 30%, Kitchen (Unit 3) 25%, Kitchen (Unit 4) 20%, Living Room (Unit 3) 20%, Exterior 10%, Entry 0%
- Specialty drying recommended for hardwood flooring in 3 rooms
- Measurement validation failed: Hallway and Exterior missing dimensions, producing incomplete task quantities
- Unit 3 rooms kept separate (Living Room ID 8, Kitchen ID 9) unlike Trace 4's merged approach -- the separate treatment better matches the floor plan structure
- Antimicrobial and decontamination tasks generated for all affected rooms (Category 2 protocol)
- Total area discrepancy: 229.2 SF

**User:** tylerhelms@winmarkelowna.com | **Time:** 1m 26s
**Rooms:** 5 total (4 affected, 1 unaffected) | **Photos:** 0 | **Notes:** 0 | **Floor Plans:** 0
**Input Quality:** 1/5 Poor | **Pipeline Health:** 2/5 Significant | **Issue Score:** 3/5 Moderate | **Overall:** 2.0/5

### What Was Provided

| Category | Status | Details |
|----------|--------|---------|
| Structures | Unknown | -- |
| Rooms | Good | 5 total; 4 affected, 1 unaffected; 0 organizational |
| Room Setup | Poor | 5 rooms; 0 in app, 4 from notes |
| Field Photos | None | 0 photos; 3 rooms without photos |
| Thermal Images | Unknown | -- |
| 360 Photos | Unknown | -- |
| Video Transcripts | Unknown | -- |
| General Notes | Unknown | -- |
| Technician Notes | None | 0 notes; 0 rooms without notes |
| Floor Plans | None | 0 plans; 0 rooms with measurements |
| Room Name Matching | N/A | 0 unmatched floor plan rooms |
| Moisture Data | None | -- |
| Guidelines | None | -- |

### Input Assessment

No field photos were submitted for this scope. No technician notes were included, so the scope relied entirely on photos and floor plans for damage details. No floor plans were uploaded, so room measurements are not available and task quantities will need to be filled in manually. Rooms were not set up in the app before running the scope, which means they will not sync back to the field application. No moisture monitoring data was submitted.

### Pipeline Assessment

The scope failed after 1m 26s. 9 of 14 processing steps completed before the failure.

### Issue Assessment

The system detected 13 data quality issue(s): 5 measurement validation warning(s); 5 room(s) with missing measurements; 3 affected room(s) without photos.

### Recommendations

1. Take photos of each affected room before running the scope. Photos help verify damage type and extent in each room.
2. Add technician notes describing the damage in each room. Notes provide details that photos alone cannot capture, like the source of water or hidden damage.
3. Upload a floor plan with room measurements. Without measurements, task quantities cannot be calculated and must be filled in manually.
4. Set up rooms in the app before running the scope. Rooms created in the app sync back to your field application; rooms inferred from notes do not.

No field photos were submitted for this scope. No technician notes were included, so the scope relied entirely on photos and floor plans for damage details. No floor plans were uploaded, so room measurements are not available and task quantities will need to be filled in manually.

### Pipeline Assessment

The scope failed after 3m 48s. 2 of 14 processing steps completed before the failure.

### Issue Assessment

No data quality issues were detected in this scope.

### Recommendations

1. Take photos of each affected room before running the scope. Photos help verify damage type and extent in each room.
2. Add technician notes describing the damage in each room. Notes provide details that photos alone cannot capture, like the source of water or hidden damage.
3. Upload a floor plan with room measurements. Without measurements, task quantities cannot be calculated and must be filled in manually.

| 360 Photos | Unknown | -- |
| Video Transcripts | Unknown | -- |
| General Notes | Unknown | -- |
| Technician Notes | None | 0 notes; 0 rooms without notes |
| Floor Plans | None | 0 plans; 0 rooms with measurements |
| Room Name Matching | N/A | 0 unmatched floor plan rooms |
| Moisture Data | None | -- |

### Input Assessment

No field photos were submitted for this scope. No technician notes were included, so the scope relied entirely on photos and floor plans for damage details. No floor plans were uploaded, so room measurements are not available and task quantities will need to be filled in manually. Rooms were not set up in the app before running the scope, which means they will not sync back to the field application. No moisture monitoring data was submitted.

### Pipeline Assessment

The scope failed after 1m 1s. 11 of 14 processing steps completed before the failure.

### Issue Assessment

The system detected 3 data quality issue(s): 1 equipment sizing conflict(s); 1 measurement validation warning(s); 1 affected room(s) without photos.

### Recommendations

1. Take photos of each affected room before running the scope. Photos help verify damage type and extent in each room.
2. Add technician notes describing the damage in each room. Notes provide details that photos alone cannot capture, like the source of water or hidden damage.
3. Upload a floor plan with room measurements. Without measurements, task quantities cannot be calculated and must be filled in manually.
4. Set up rooms in the app before running the scope. Rooms created in the app sync back to your field application; rooms inferred from notes do not.

3. Set up rooms in the app before running the scope. Rooms created in the app sync back to your field application; rooms inferred from notes do not.

### Recommendations

2. Add notes for the 3 room(s) missing them. Even brief notes about damage type and extent help.
3. Check that floor plan room labels match the room names in your claim. Mismatched names prevent measurements from being assigned.
